{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Warehouse Officer",
  "description": "Erisco Foods Limited is an indigenous food product manufacturer, incorporated in Nigeria in 2004 and started operations in 2009, manufacturing Tomato Paste, Seasoning, Rice, Milk cubes, and more. We are firmly committed to producing high-quality products that combine nourishment with value pricing, as we strive to become one of Nigeria&rsquo;s largest indigenous food manufacturers on a global scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Iju Ishaga, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Erisco Foods Limited is seeking a diligent and organized Warehouse Officer to join our team in Iju Ishaga.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing warehouse operations, ensuring efficient inventory control, proper documentation, and timely dispatch of goods.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming goods and materials.
	Organize and maintain inventory according to company standards.
	Ensure accurate documentation of stock movement (inflow and outflow).
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages or excesses.
	Coordinate the loading and offloading of goods.
	Maintain cleanliness and safety standards in the warehouse.
	Support periodic stock-taking and reconciliation exercises.
	Work closely with the logistics and sales teams to ensure timely delivery


Requirements


	Minimum of an OND or equivalent in Logistics, Business Administration, or related field.
	At least 2 years of relevant experience in a warehouse or inventory management role.
	Strong organizational and time management skills.
	Ability to work with minimal supervision.
	Familiarity with inventory software is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@eriscofoodsltd.com.ng&nbsp;using &quot;Warehouse Officer - Iju Ishaga&rdquo; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualifed candidates will be contacted.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-03",

Warehouse Officer Job at Erisco Foods Limited




        
    

        
        Erisco Foods Limited is an indigenous food product manufacturer, incorporated in Nigeria in 2004 and started operations in 2009, manufacturing Tomato Paste, Seasoning, Rice, Milk cubes, and more. We are firmly committed to producing high-quality products that combine nourishment with value pricing, as we strive to become one of Nigeria’s largest indigenous food manufacturers on a global scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Officer

Job Location: Iju Ishaga, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Erisco Foods Limited is seeking a diligent and organized Warehouse Officer to join our team in Iju Ishaga.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing warehouse operations, ensuring efficient inventory control, proper documentation, and timely dispatch of goods.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming goods and materials.
	Organize and maintain inventory according to company standards.
	Ensure accurate documentation of stock movement (inflow and outflow).
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages or excesses.
	Coordinate the loading and offloading of goods.
	Maintain cleanliness and safety standards in the warehouse.
	Support periodic stock-taking and reconciliation exercises.
	Work closely with the logistics and sales teams to ensure timely delivery


Requirements


	Minimum of an OND or equivalent in Logistics, Business Administration, or related field.
	At least 2 years of relevant experience in a warehouse or inventory management role.
	Strong organizational and time management skills.
	Ability to work with minimal supervision.
	Familiarity with inventory software is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Warehouse Officer - Iju Ishaga” as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualifed candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Site Manager",
  "description": "The Startup Place Limited is a Business Consulting firm with key focus on Startup Development, Digital Marketing and Business Growth. We pull a team of passionate entrepreneurs and consultant about helping you start, grow and scale your business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily activities across project sites to ensure work is executed according to design specifications and project timelines.
	Monitor contractors, artisans, and suppliers to ensure quality standards are maintained.
	Conduct routine site inspections and provide updates on project progress to the Project Manager and Design Team.
	Ensure compliance with safety procedures and best practices across project sites.
	Liaise with vendors, artisans, and suppliers to coordinate deliveries, installations, and site requirements.
	Coordinate the collection, dispatch, and delivery of materials, fittings, and accessories required for project execution.
	Maintain accurate records of items collected, delivered, or dispatched across project sites.
	Serve as the primary on-site point of contact between the design team, vendors, contractors, and other stakeholders.
	Document site progress with photographs, videos, and written updates for internal reporting and project tracking.
	Escalate delays, material shortages, or on-site challenges promptly to the Project Manager.
	Assist with quality control checks for furniture, fittings, and installations.
	Support inventory management and storage of materials and accessories across project sites.
	Assist with site preparation activities before client handover or project photoshoots.
	Other responsibilities as assigned by management.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Construction, Building Technology, Civil Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or any related field.
	Minimum of 1&ndash;2 years of professional experience in procurement, construction, site supervision, interior design project execution, or a related role.
	Ability to manage multiple site activities and meet project deadlines.
	Ability to work effectively with contractors, artisans, and vendors.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced project environment.
	Strong site coordination and supervision skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send CVs to:&nbsp;hr@thestartupplaceng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-29",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {

Site Manager Job at the Startup Place Limited




        
    

        
        The Startup Place Limited is a Business Consulting firm with key focus on Startup Development, Digital Marketing and Business Growth. We pull a team of passionate entrepreneurs and consultant about helping you start, grow and scale your business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee daily activities across project sites to ensure work is executed according to design specifications and project timelines.
	Monitor contractors, artisans, and suppliers to ensure quality standards are maintained.
	Conduct routine site inspections and provide updates on project progress to the Project Manager and Design Team.
	Ensure compliance with safety procedures and best practices across project sites.
	Liaise with vendors, artisans, and suppliers to coordinate deliveries, installations, and site requirements.
	Coordinate the collection, dispatch, and delivery of materials, fittings, and accessories required for project execution.
	Maintain accurate records of items collected, delivered, or dispatched across project sites.
	Serve as the primary on-site point of contact between the design team, vendors, contractors, and other stakeholders.
	Document site progress with photographs, videos, and written updates for internal reporting and project tracking.
	Escalate delays, material shortages, or on-site challenges promptly to the Project Manager.
	Assist with quality control checks for furniture, fittings, and installations.
	Support inventory management and storage of materials and accessories across project sites.
	Assist with site preparation activities before client handover or project photoshoots.
	Other responsibilities as assigned by management.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Construction, Building Technology, Civil Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or any related field.
	Minimum of 1–2 years of professional experience in procurement, construction, site supervision, interior design project execution, or a related role.
	Ability to manage multiple site activities and meet project deadlines.
	Ability to work effectively with contractors, artisans, and vendors.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced project environment.
	Strong site coordination and supervision skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "",
  "description": "Intersect Consortium is a world-class organization that provides services and products for high-quality mental health, technology and medical equipment sales. Our purpose is to make a timely and long-lasting difference in the lives of individuals and families we support. Our treatment programs have mainly been developed by clinicians working in America, United Kingdom, and Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.)&nbsp;Driver

Job Location:&nbsp;Jahi, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Driver will assist the company with all transport-related duties and will be responsible for conveying staff of the organization, materials and equipment to and from where they may be needed in a timely manner.


Responsibilities


	Safely transporting company staff and various products to and from specified locations.
	Plan travel routes to avoid traffic congestion or road construction.
	Inspect vehicles for mechanical items and safety issues and perform preventive maintenance where necessary.
	Keep the company vehicle clean and properly maintained.
	Document and log work/rest periods and kilometers spent driving and retain fuel/toll receipts.
	Comply with road safety rules and regulations as well as with company policies and procedures.
	Collect and verify delivery instructions.
	Report damage, accident or injuries to management.


Requirements and Experience


	Candidates should possess a First School Leaving Certificate (FSLC).
	3 - 7 years of work experience.
	Proven work experience as a driver in a corporate organization.
	No recent driving violations.
	Adaptability and foresight to handle unexpected situations (traffics, weather conditions etc.)
	Ability to drive long hours and occasional travels
	Willing to submit to background / drug checks and provide employment recommendations
	Valid driving license.
	Familiar with Abuja routes.
	Must be a resident of Abuja.


Salary
N80,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date:&nbsp;12th May, 2026.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@intersectconsortium.com&nbsp;using &quot;Driver&quot; as the subject of the email.

&nbsp;

&nbsp;

2.)&nbsp;Business Development Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for an experienced and strategic Business Development Manager to take the lead on all marketing and business development activities. To excel in this role, you need to have strong leadership skills and manage the team to drive and generate revenue opportunities for the company.


Job Description
Product Knowledge:


	Develop a sound understanding of all products and value propositions and continuously expand knowledge of Products.
	Conduct presentations and be able to demonstrate Product functionalities completely and accurately.
	Work closely with Product, Engineering, and UX teams to provide feedback, share customer insights, and influence the product roadmap.
	Understand the technical architecture and underlying technology to confidently engage both technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Business Development:


	Think strategically, work strategically and act strategically.
	Develop a Business Development Strategy to achieve short &amp; long term goals and increase client base.
	Proactively identify &amp; harness new business opportunities and potential clients in the public and private sector.
	Write, prepare and submit Business Proposals.
	Work with the Finance Team to develop pricing proposals.
	Build and retain long-term relationships with existing clients, stakeholders, and industry professionals.
	Develop relationships and network with key clients and senior decision-makers in government Agencies, Private organisations, and other relevant influencers.
	Develop goals and sales targets for the development team and ensure they are met.
	Work with the Social Media Team in content Creation, and using Social Media to drive business growth.
	Brainstorm, think strategically, and develop an approach to close deals and convert leads to customers.


Other:


	Arrange business meetings and one-on-one conversations with prospective clients.
	Work with the IT Department to ensure Customer Product satisfaction.
	Provide periodic feedback to the product development team.
	Prepare Monthly and Annual Reports that track business development activities and measure performance with appropriate KPIs and metrics.
	Seek ways of improving the way the business operates.
	Perform other related duties as assigned.


Requirements


	Minimum of a Master&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing or related field with&nbsp;5 &ndash; 7 years of cognate experience.
	Experience working in an IT Company will be an added advantage.
	Proven track record of launching, selling, or scaling technology products (B2B, B2C, or SaaS preferred).
	Existing network within the tech ecosystem, government agencies, or innovation hubs.
	Capacity to manage high-stress situations.
	Ability to multitask, manage and independently lead various projects simultaneously.
	Excellent verbal and written Communication Skills
	Strong work ethic.
	Strong interpersonal skills with experience in building rapport and creating strong relationships.
	Ability to work with minimal supervision, prioritize work and meet deadlines.
	Able to grasp technical and product-related concepts quickly and accurately.


Application Closing Date:&nbsp;28th April, 2026.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;hr@intersectconsortium.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

&nbsp;

&nbsp;

3.)&nbsp;Senior Internal Audit Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job description


	Plans financial, regulatory, compliance or operational reviews/audits.
	Coordinates work with Risk, Legal &amp; Compliance and other control-related activities and with others within Internal Audit.
	Conducts financial risk assessments and identifies controls in place to mitigate identified risks.
	Ensure daily compliance on the internal and financial policies of the facility
	Do a daily call over on all expenses across all organizations and ensure that all postings are correctly done
	Takes routine stocks check and verify that records in the bin cards show true representation of stock on ground
	Ensure that costs are not over stated on all expenses.
	Ensure compliance on all regulatory policies.
	Ensure that internal control system is in place so as to control loss, theft and risk in the facility.
	Identifies any internal control gap and recommend measures for its improvement.
	Report to management all risk issues in accordance with auditing standards.
	Review the final accounts prepared by the accountant before the external audit reviews the account.
	Advices management on any internal control issue.
	Performs audit procedures to verify that controls are operating through testing and interviewing techniques.
	Analyses and concludes on effectiveness and efficiency of control environment.
	Identifies control gaps and opportunities for improvement.
	Documents the results of audit work in accordance with audit department and the Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA) standards.
	Prepares timely audit reports for executive management, the Audit Committee and the Board of Directors.
	Assess, evaluate and promote compliance to internal policies.
	Contributes, as appropriate, in the year-end financial audit with the external auditor.
	Provides advice on internal control and participates in enhancing internal audit standards and practices within Synapse
	Researches new or technical subjects when required to support audits (e.g., OSFI Guidelines, market risk, financial instruments, etc.)
	Provide feedback on Accounting Procedures.


Deliverables / Key Performance Indicator


	Monthly Report/ Advise on Return on investment of Synapse
	Continuous audit tests and their results Monthly
	Good number of managements agreed actions outstanding, putting emphasis on any overdue actions. This is related to the financial advice rendered by the internal auditor.
	Policy and procedure breaches reported in the period: Weekly Report
	Emerging risk discovery: forensic auditing. Monthly Review
	Status of compliance test completion and report of any failures with their remediation plans
	Monthly Rich content of audit report based on proper research and analysis
	Above all, customer satisfaction without compromising on objectivity
	Completed audit plans per year or period
	Business or revenue enhancement opportunities were identified and proposed as cost-saving strategies.


Requirements


	B.Sc. Business or Accounting.
	Knowledge of economic and accounting principles and practices, analysis, and reporting of financial data.
	Direct experience in audit and compliance is required. Good knowledge in auditing and accounting practices.
	ACCA/ICAN / ACA / CIA / CISA.
	Minimum of 5 years of proven work experience.
	Relevant certifications are associated with the job position.
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and influencing skills; ability to negotiate at all levels.


Competencies/Attributes:


	Contributes to High Performing Teams &ndash; May include supervision of Accountant and account assistant on projects to follow, sound internal control practices and to manage financial risks appropriately.
	Acts as a Team Player - Collaborate and supporting colleagues and peers across the organization, while still being able to work independently when needed.
	Results Oriented &ndash; Meet deadlines on assignments, juggle multiple demands and to work with all types of individuals up to and including the CEO.
	Entrepreneurial
	Committed to Self-Development Requirements:
	Excellent knowledge of healthcare financial business operations and risk-based auditing attained through 3 to 5 years of progressive work experience with a focus on financial risk and regulatory requirements.
	Excellent understanding of the standards of the Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA) and ability to fully comply with IIA standards
	Ability to manage projects and, when required, work with the accountant to follow sound internal control practices and to manage risks appropriately.
	Strong skills in negotiating, relationship building, problem solving, and timely problem escalation.


Application Closing Date:&nbsp;8th May, 2026.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;hr@intersectconsortium.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&quot;Internal Audit Executive&quot; as the subject of the email.

Job Opening at Intersect Consortium




        
    

        
        Intersect Consortium is a world-class organization that provides services and products for high-quality mental health, technology and medical equipment sales. Our purpose is to make a timely and long-lasting difference in the lives of individuals and families we support. Our treatment programs have mainly been developed by clinicians working in America, United Kingdom, and Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Driver

Job Location: Jahi, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary

	The Driver will assist the company with all transport-related duties and will be responsible for conveying staff of the organization, materials and equipment to and from where they may be needed in a timely manner.

	Plan travel routes to avoid traffic congestion or road construction.
	Inspect vehicles for mechanical items and safety issues and perform preventive maintenance where necessary.
	Keep the company vehicle clean and properly maintained.
	Document and log work/rest periods and kilometers spent driving and retain fuel/toll receipts.
	Comply with road safety rules and regulations as well as with company policies and procedures.
	Collect and verify delivery instructions.
	Report damage, accident or injuries to management.


Requirements and Experience


	Candidates should possess a First School Leaving Certificate (FSLC).
	3 - 7 years of work experience.
	Proven work experience as a driver in a corporate organization.
	No recent driving violations.
	Adaptability and foresight to handle unexpected situations (traffics, weather conditions etc.)
	Ability to drive long hours and occasional travels
	Willing to submit to background / drug checks and provide employment recommendations
	Valid driving license.
	Familiar with Abuja routes.
	Must be a resident of Abuja.


Salary
N80,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 12th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Driver" as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": ".NET Core Developer",
  "description": "Stretch-it Concepts - Our client, an IT Company in Lagos, is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: .NET Core Developer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Available Slot: 2

Job&nbsp;Summary


	You will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining secure, high-performance web applications, APIs, and backend systems that power business operations and digital products.


Key&nbsp;Responsibilities


	Develop and maintain web applications using .NET Core / ASP.NET Core
	Build and integrate RESTful APIs
	Write clean, scalable, and maintainable code
	Optimize applications for speed, security, and reliability
	Work with front-end developers to integrate user-facing elements
	Troubleshoot bugs and improve system performance
	Design and manage databases (SQL Server / MySQL)
	Participate in code reviews and technical planning
	Collaborate with product, QA, and design teams when required


What&nbsp;We&nbsp;Are&nbsp;Looking&nbsp;For


	B.Sc / HND in Computer Science or related field
	2+ years of experience with .NET Core / C# development
	Good knowledge of ASP.NET Core MVC / Web API
	Strong understanding of SQL databases
	Familiarity with Git / version control
	Understanding of software architecture and best practices
	Problem-solving mindset and teamwork skills.


Why&nbsp;You&nbsp;Should&nbsp;Apply


	Work on exciting real-world software projects
	Join a growing and innovative tech company
	Career growth and learning opportunities
	Collaborative and modern work environment
	Competitive compensation software.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to:&nbsp;jobs@stretchitconcepts.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;NET CORE DEVELOPER&quot; as the subject of the email.",

.NET Core Developer Job at Stretch-it Concepts




        
    

        
        Stretch-it Concepts - Our client, an IT Company in Lagos, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: .NET Core Developer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Available Slot: 2

Job Summary


	You will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining secure, high-performance web applications, APIs, and backend systems that power business operations and digital products.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and maintain web applications using .NET Core / ASP.NET Core
	Build and integrate RESTful APIs
	Write clean, scalable, and maintainable code
	Optimize applications for speed, security, and reliability
	Work with front-end developers to integrate user-facing elements
	Troubleshoot bugs and improve system performance
	Design and manage databases (SQL Server / MySQL)
	Participate in code reviews and technical planning
	Collaborate with product, QA, and design teams when required


What We Are Looking For


	B.Sc / HND in Computer Science or related field
	2+ years of experience with .NET Core / C# development
	Good knowledge of ASP.NET Core MVC / Web API
	Strong understanding of SQL databases
	Familiarity with Git / version control
	Understanding of software architecture and best practices
	Problem-solving mindset and teamwork skills.


Why You Should Apply


	Work on exciting real-world software projects
	Join a growing and innovative tech company
	Career growth and learning opportunities
	Collaborative and modern work environment
	Competitive compensation software.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using “NET CORE DEVELOPER" as the subject of the email.
